title: Back up pre-consolidation registry
next_action: Use backup only for rollback or comparison.
acceptance: plans/registry.backup-2026-05-04.json exists and contains the pre-rewrite registry.

title: Rewrite registry to four active parent plans
next_action: Keep only these four records in active plan views.
acceptance: plans/registry.json has active_parent_ids for the four parent plans and .plans contains those four active records.

title: Promote consolidated tasks into task ledger
next_action: Use this ledger as the operating checklist instead of old wave docs.
acceptance: tasks/tasks.jsonl contains promoted records with plan_id, priority, runner, status, next_action, acceptance, and proof_required.

title: Create active consolidation run record
next_action: Use the run record for report-back and continuation.
acceptance: runs/runs.jsonl contains a consolidation run with target and current step.

title: Create proof ledger records for consolidation state
next_action: Attach new proof as implementation continues.
acceptance: proofs/proof-ledger.jsonl contains proof records for registry rewrite, task promotion, run record, Drive sync status correction, and open workflow/visualizer status.

title: Regenerate FAMTASTIC-STATUS.md from consolidated state
next_action: Automate this later from registry and ledgers.
acceptance: FAMTASTIC-STATUS.md names the four active parent plans and current open P0/P1/P2 work.

title: Rebuild Workbench against frozen UI foundation
next_action: Audit current Workbench prototype against docs/STUDIO-UI-FOUNDATION.md and patch the shell/workspace data without breaking launchd-managed Studio.
acceptance: Workbench no longer presents generic card-first workspace direction and every visible mode follows the frozen workspace contract.

title: Declare workspace contracts for all top-level domains
next_action: Make Build, Plan, Components, Media Studio, Research, and Deploy contract data explicit and consume it in the Workbench UI.
acceptance: Each workspace declares domain, route, parent, purpose, primary surface, selected object model, tools, Shay context, proof, promotion targets, and anti-patterns.

title: Make Media Studio prompt-first
next_action: Replace any media-library-first workspace framing with a prompt-first creation surface and supportive library/tools.
acceptance: Media Studio center surface is generation/comparison/approval/promotion first; library appears as supporting access, not the domain identity.

title: Wire Workbench Plan mode to real registry/task/run/proof data
next_action: Keep site-studio/public/data/workbench-plan-state.json regenerated until automatic status export replaces it.
acceptance: Plan mode shows the same four parent plans, task statuses, current run, proof links, and blockers that fam-hub reports.

title: Register Workbench as Shay context provider
next_action: Expose domain, route, selected object, selected tool, proof state, and open plan/run to Shay.
acceptance: A Shay call can see the current Workbench context and answer/action against it without guessing.
proof_required: actual Shay-Shay call through Studio UI

title: Add fam-hub plan review
next_action: Use fam-hub plan review as the registry/ledger drift check.
acceptance: fam-hub plan review reports active count, absorbed records, empty/missing ledgers, stale statuses, and next actions.

title: Add fam-hub task promote
next_action: Use fam-hub task promote --dry-run before future apply runs.
acceptance: fam-hub task promote can dry-run and apply task promotions with stable task IDs and duplicate detection.

title: Add fam-hub run start
next_action: Use fam-hub run start <plan-id> [target] for new active runs.
acceptance: fam-hub run start appends a valid JSONL run and fam-hub run status shows it.

title: Build first usable knowledge-capture pass
next_action: Create an ingest/extract/propose flow for designated conversations and docs with no canonical writes until approval.
acceptance: A capture run can extract decisions, gaps, lessons, contradictions, and proposed destinations into a reviewable packet.

title: Declare BuildIntent v2 current
next_action: Write the supersession record that makes v2 current and v1 archived/evidence.
acceptance: Canonical docs and registry no longer treat v1/v2 as ambiguous active plans.

title: Instrument parallelBuild before refactor
next_action: Add stage boundary, input, output, duration, and proof-event logging without changing build execution order.
acceptance: A real Studio build emits traceable workflow events sufficient to explain stage flow and fulfillment status.

title: Start workflow-as-data phase 1
next_action: Define stage names, boundaries, inputs, outputs, and proof events as data while leaving execution imperative.
acceptance: A declarative stage catalog exists and current code logs against it before any stage migration.

title: Build pipeline visualizer inspect/trace/propose
next_action: Pipeline visualizer phase 1 is implemented in Workbench Plan mode with inspect, trace, and propose lanes backed by the workflow catalog and trace API.
acceptance: Visualizer shows inspect, trace, and propose functions backed by real run data; reorder/swap/compare/history remain later.

title: Define three site workflows
next_action: Make new_site_from_brief, adapt_existing_site, and rebuild_from_brief first-class workflow modes with separate state/config expectations.
acceptance: Workflow definitions state inputs, permissions, config discovery, build path, proof, and gaps for all three modes.

title: Create MBSH site-scoped task records
next_action: Break backend, RSVP, sponsor, media, chatbot, deploy, content, and proof into explicit site tasks.
acceptance: MBSH tasks are visible separately from platform tasks and reusable gaps have promotion targets.

title: Separate MBSH deploy repo state from Studio state
next_action: Verify current deploy repo, Studio tag, symlink/preview path, Netlify/GitHub status, and document mismatches.
acceptance: A status note names source of truth for deploy repo, Studio preview, backend, DNS, and pending cutover work.

title: Use MBSH as Studio audit harness
next_action: MBSH reproduction audit harness is documented with exact prompt, verification method, current proof boundary, and reusable gaps.
acceptance: Audit outputs both a site verdict and a backlog of reusable Studio improvements.

title: Verify MBSH backend endpoint inventory
next_action: Test PHP endpoints, schema, env needs, and local/staging assumptions before production deploy.
acceptance: Task has site-scoped proof and any reusable system gap is promoted upward instead of staying buried in MBSH notes.

acceptance: Task has site-scoped proof and any reusable system gap is promoted upward instead of staying buried in MBSH notes.

title: Verify MBSH sponsor flow
next_action: Sponsor browser submission path is verified and frontend anti-bot guard is fixed; remaining backend runtime smoke test is blocked by missing config/secrets and is tracked under deploy proof.
acceptance: Task has site-scoped proof and any reusable system gap is promoted upward instead of staying buried in MBSH notes.

title: Finish MBSH deploy proof
next_action: Bootstrap Site Studio service auth, provision-check MBSH as a generated site consumer, generate production backend config/API_BASE_URL from Studio service state, add cPanel DNS/addon-domain automation or complete the provider UI fallback, then run the documented smoke and rollback proof.
acceptance: MBSH deploy proof treats Studio/platform as the provider owner and the site as a consumer; reusable service auth gaps are promoted upward instead of staying buried in MBSH notes.
blockers: Site Studio service auth/provisioning is incomplete for production API_BASE_URL, cPanel DNS/addon-domain automation coverage, and SSH host trust.
MBSH backend secrets/config must be generated by Studio/platform, not hand-owned by the site.
Live external smoke tests remain blocked until Studio-managed services are provisioned.

next_action: Chatbot Phase 1 frontend is browser-verified; backend persistence/email smoke remains part of deploy/runtime config proof.
acceptance: Task has site-scoped proof and any reusable system gap is promoted upward instead of staying buried in MBSH notes.

acceptance: Task has site-scoped proof and any reusable system gap is promoted upward instead of staying buried in MBSH notes.

acceptance: Task has site-scoped proof and any reusable system gap is promoted upward instead of staying buried in MBSH notes.

acceptance: Task has site-scoped proof and any reusable system gap is promoted upward instead of staying buried in MBSH notes.

next_action: Use this proof pattern for future Shay behavior tests; do not replace with direct API or WebSocket shortcuts.
